mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 2% signal aggressive monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings. This mechanism operates continuously as a trend signal without requiring threshold activation.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.33% as of March 2009 reflects recent aggressive Fed cuts from 5.25% peak in June 2008
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate crosses above 4.5% threshold, signaling labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns as equity valuations contract on lower earnings expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.8% in March 2009 but rising sharply from 4.7% in October 2008
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE > 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly decline, indicating synchronized contraction in consumer and residential demand. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative equity returns as forward earnings guidance deteriorates across consumer-sensitive sectors.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month AND HOUST falls below 1000 thousand units
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 697557M and HOUST at 1283K as of February 2009, both in severe contraction
regime_hint: synchronized demand shock in consumer and housing sectors
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index (INDPRO) falls below 103 threshold, indicating manufacturing contraction that signals broader economic weakness and reduced corporate profitability. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative 5-day returns as equity markets reprice earnings downward.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.49 in February 2009, near crisis lows
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 65% of instances where INDPRO < 103.0, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) declines month-over-month, signaling labor shedding that reduces aggregate income and consumer spending capacity. This mechanism operates as an always-on trend signal predicting negative equity returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 156874K in March 2009, down sharply from 157745K in January 2009
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 58% of months with declining PAYEMS, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) declines month-over-month below 307.0 index level, indicating deflationary pressure that increases real debt burdens and reduces nominal earnings growth expectations. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts negative returns in deflationary environments.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 307.48 in March 2009, showing deflationary pressure from crisis
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 62% of deflationary months, or correlation between CPIAUCSL momentum and SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.12).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) both show month-over-month acceleration after sustained decline, indicating demand stabilization that signals end of crisis phase. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive 20-day returns as equity markets price in recovery expectations.
candidate_trigger: HOUST increases > 5% month-over-month AND RSAFS increases > 1% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Both series show stabilization signals in early 2009
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of instances where both HOUST and RSAFS accelerate, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) both decline simultaneously, indicating synchronized supply-side and labor contraction that predicts severe earnings deterioration. This interaction-driven mechanism produces negative 60-day returns as equity markets reprice downside risks.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 1% month-over-month AND PAYEMS declines > 50 thousand persons month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Both series in sharp contraction through early 2009
regime_hint: synchronized supply and labor shock regime
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient lacks statistical significance (p > 0.15) in multivariate specification.
